audience_docs.excerpt: Hush Line Use Cases This document rationalizes Hush Line's use cases with AGENTS.md, the current application surface, and the public/library documentation. The goal is to keep product thinking grounded in the people Hush Line actually serves, the workflows the app currently supports, and the safety/privacy constraints that shape those workflows. Grounding Primary grounding reference: docs/ISO 37002.md Product principles: Usability of the Software Authenticity of the Receiver Plausible Deniability of the Whistleblower Availability of the System Anonymity of the Whistleblower Confidentiality and Integrity of the Disclosures Primary User Groups | Group | Typical Users | What They Need From Hush Line | | | | | | Message Senders | Whistleblowers, concerned citizens, engaged citizens, activists, students, bug bounty hunters | A low friction, privacy preserving way to find a trustworthy recipient and send a message without creating an account | | Message Recipients | Journalists, newsrooms, documentary teams, lawyers, law firms, employers, boards, educators, school administrators, organizers, activists, software developers, security teams, nonprofits | A trustworthy intake channel, secure delivery path, public credibility signals, and a manageable workflow for reviewing and responding to messages | | Shared or Role Based Recipients | Board inboxes, ethics/compliance contacts, public accountability channels, legal intake teams, security reporting addresses | Shared intake endpoints that can be published publicly without forcing a single personal identity | | Platform Administrators | Internal operators for managed or single tenant deployments | Branding, governance controls, registration gates, trust management, and account moderation | Deployment Models | Model | Best Fit | Wh…

Welcome to Hush Line Source basis: the public Hush Line Library welcome section and the project overview in README.md. Hush Line is an open source whistleblower platform for secure, anonymous, one way disclosures. It is built for journalists, lawyers, educators, organizers, employers, and other trusted recipients who need a safer way to receive tips from their communities. What Hush Line is designed to protect Anonymous submissions without requiring a sender account End to end encrypted delivery when the receiver has a PGP key Trust building signals such as verified accounts and public profile details Safer access paths, including Tor support and privacy preserving defaults Common ways people use it Publish a public tip line for your newsroom, law office, classroom, campaign, or company Receive messages in a web inbox without turning the sender flow into a live chat Share a directory listing or direct profile link so sources can verify they reached the right person Run the hosted service or use a Personal Server Where to begin New hosted service users should start with Start Here. If you want the guided setup flow, open New User Onboarding. If you already have an account, jump to Prep Your Account and Secure Your Account.

Hush Line Hush Line is an open source whistleblower platform for secure, anonymous, one way disclosures to journalists, lawyers, and other trusted recipients. Hosted service: <https://tips.hushline.app Start here: <https://hushline.app/library/docs/getting started/start here/ Why Hush Line Hush Line is built for safety critical reporting workflows where trust, anonymity, and usability all matter. The project design priorities are: Usability of the software Authenticity of the receiver Plausible deniability of the whistleblower Availability of the system Anonymity of the whistleblower Confidentiality and integrity of disclosures Core Capabilities | Area | What Hush Line Provides | | | | | Anonymous submissions | No submitter account required for sending disclosures | | Encryption | End to end encryption workflow with recipient PGP keys, plus server side fallback path | | Receiver trust | Verified account workflow and trusted directory UX | | Account security | Password authentication with optional TOTP 2FA | | Privacy access | Tor onion support and privacy preserving defaults | | Communication workflow | Message status management, one way replies, and optional email forwarding modes | | Org customization | Branding controls, onboarding guidance, and configurable profile fields | | Operational controls | Strong CI checks, migration compatibility testing, and workflow security validation | Quickstart (Local) 1) Clone and start Open <http://localhost:8080 . If you only want to start the current stack without first tearing it down, you can still use docker compose up or make run . If you want a slower, guided setup for a brand new machine, use the AI ready prompt in docs/LOCAL CONTRIBUTOR ONBOARDING PROMPT.md .

Prep Your Account Source: <https://hushline.app/library/docs/getting started/account setup/ Preparing your account means getting your profile, encryption, and message delivery settings into a state you are comfortable publishing. 1. Fill in your public profile At minimum, add: a recognizable display name an optional account category that matches the type of recipient you are a short bio any extra profile details you want sources to trust, such as a website or Signal handle If you opt into the public directory, this is the information whistleblowers will use to decide whether your tip line belongs to the right person. 2. Add encryption If tips will be delivered to an email address, PGP setup is the important step. Supported options: Proton Mail key lookup manual public key paste Without a PGP key, people can still see your profile, but they cannot submit tips through it. 3. Decide how messages should reach you Hush Line can notify you about new tips in the web inbox and, optionally, by email. In Settings you can: enable or disable email notifications add multiple notification recipients when one shared account needs to alert a small team include encrypted message content in those emails encrypt the entire email body for compatibility with clients such as Proton Mail or Thunderbird Each notification recipient can use a different delivery address and PGP key. This lets a newsroom, nonprofit, or legal intake team share one public account while keeping each operator's mail workflow separate. 4. Review your public form If you use custom message fields, decide which ones are: required or optional encrypted or unencrypted Unencrypted custom fields are visible to you and your hosting provider. Encrypted fields require your PGP workflow to read them. 5.

The Hush Line Personal Server Source: <https://hushline.app/library/docs/personal server/the hush line personal server/ The Hush Line Personal Server is the self hosted Hush Line appliance. It is designed for people who want the Hush Line workflow on hardware they control while still giving sources an anonymous, browser based submission path. How access works The Personal Server presents two addresses: a Tor onion address for private access through Tor Browser a local network HTTPS address for access from the network the device is connected to The in app server info page shows both addresses directly on the device. First run setup 1. Visit the device address. 2. Register the first account. 3. The first user created on a Personal Server becomes the admin. 4. Add your profile, encryption key, and notification settings. 5. Share the onion URL publicly so sources can reach you anonymously. Who it is for The product copy describes the Personal Server as a good fit for lawyers, journalists, educators, and business leaders who want a safer reporting channel for their communities. Related docs Specs Secure Your Account Share Your Tip Line

Public Record Provenance Roadmap (U.S.) This artifact tracks the active U.S. implementation roadmap and the policy only EU scaffold used to open follow on country issues. Scope Build an authoritative, ever growing U.S. public record attorney dataset. Keep only listings with strict, per record official source URLs. Remove listings when the official record can no longer be verified. Current Baseline (March 10, 2026) Active strict listings: 58 States with strict listings: AK , AL , AR , AZ , CA , CO , CT , DE , GA , IA , ID , IL , IN , KS , MA , MD , ME , MI , MN , MO , MS , MT , NC , ND , NE , NH , NJ , NM , OH , RI , SC , SD , TN , TX , UT , WA Other U.S. rows were removed because they only pointed to generic directory pages (not the exact record URL). Strict Provenance Rules source url must point to the exact public record URL for that listing. source url must be on the expected official state authority domain. Generic source pages are rejected. Synthetic markers ( listing= query/fragment hacks) are rejected. Chambers/private rankings are rejected as authoritative sources. State Adapter Strategy Discovery/additions must come from explicit state adapters only. All 50 states now have explicit adapter entries in discovery code. Each adapter must: query the official state authority source resolve an exact per record URL emit normalized listing rows that pass strict validation Until a state specific extractor is implemented, that state's adapter remains a no op.

hushline_agent_context: AGENTS.md This file provides operating guidance for coding agents working in the Hush Line repository. We are a 501(c)(3) non profit based in the United States, and consider this software safety critical infrastructure protecting users’ operational, physical, and digital security. Grounding Document Primary grounding reference: docs/ISO 37002.md ( ISO 37002:2021 Whistleblowing management systems Guidelines ) Use case reference: docs/USE CASES.md (canonical product and workflow use cases for agents) Principles Usability of the Software Authenticity of the Receiver Plausible Deniability of the Whistleblower Availability of the System Anonymity of the Whistleblower Confidentiality and Integrity of the Disclosures Core Users | Message Senders | Message Recipients | Administrators | | | | | | Whistleblowers | Journalists and Newsrooms | Platform Administrators | | Concerned citizens | Lawyers and Law Offices | | | Engaged citizens | Employers and Boards | | | Activists | Educators and Administrators | | | Students | Organizers and Activists | | | Bug bounty hunters | Software developers | | Geographic Location Global; default to privacy preserving choices across jurisdictions. Scope Applies to the entire repository unless a deeper AGENTS.md exists in a subdirectory. Project Priorities Preserve core whistleblower flow above all else. Prefer behavior preserving changes over refactors unless explicitly requested. Keep privacy/security guarantees intact when editing routes, models, or settings. Accessibility score must be 100. Performance score must be =95.
hushline_app_voice_guidance: Use practical language from hushline.app: Hush Line is for anonymous, end-to-end encrypted contact and secure first contact, not broad marketing claims.
Keep the message grounded in the people Hush Line serves, such as sources, journalists, lawyers, educators, developers, organizers, and trusted recipients when the screenshot supports that audience.

Prefer concrete platform framing from hushline.app like no app download or account required for sources, a public directory that helps people find the right recipient, and browser-based tools that support real review workflows.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
Finding the right newsroom should not take guesswork.

Hush Line's public Newsrooms tab lets you search and filter newsroom listings, including by location, before you share anything sensitive. No account is required to send a tip.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Find a newsroom before you send a tip
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
If you need to reach a newsroom, finding the right one should not depend on guesswork.

Hush Line's public Newsrooms tab lets you search and filter newsroom listings in one place, including location filters, so you can narrow the field before you share anything sensitive. You do not need an account to send a message.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

Finding the right newsroom should not take guesswork.

recent_archive_history.subtext: The public Newsrooms tab helps sources search and narrow newsroom listings by location before they share anything sensitive.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
When an email looks questionable, recipients need a quick way to inspect the headers before they rely on it.

Hush Line's Email Validation tool checks DKIM, SPF, and DMARC from raw headers and lets you download a report. Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Check suspicious email headers before you act
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When a source email looks off, the first question is simple: did it really come from where it says it did?

Hush Line includes an Email Validation tool for recipients and staff. Paste raw headers, inspect DKIM, SPF, and DMARC results, and download a report you can keep with your review notes.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
When an email looks questionable, recipients need a fast way to check the headers before they rely on it.

Hush Line's Email Validation tool lets you paste raw headers, review DKIM, SPF, and DMARC results, and download a report for your records.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Hush Line includes an email validation tool so recipients can inspect raw headers, review authentication checks, and save a report.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
Protecting a tip line starts with protecting the account behind it.

Hush Line lets recipients turn on two-factor authentication from Settings and manage sign-in details in one place.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Turn on two-factor authentication for your account
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
Protecting a tip line starts with protecting the account behind it.

Hush Line lets recipients turn on two-factor authentication from Settings and manage core sign-in details in one place. That matters for journalists, lawyers, and other trusted recipients who need to keep access to sensitive inboxes under control.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

Hush Line lets recipients turn on two-factor authentication and manage sign-in details from Settings, so journalists, lawyers, and other trusted recipients can lock down inbox access.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Recipients can manage core sign-in settings and enable 2FA from one place before they start handling sensitive tips.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
Before you share your Hush Line profile, add your PGP key.

Recipients can import a Proton Mail key or paste a public key manually. Your profile can still be visible without it, but people cannot send encrypted tips until encryption is set up.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Add your PGP key before you share your tip line
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
Before you share your Hush Line profile, add your PGP key.

This screen lets recipients import a Proton Mail key or paste a public key manually. Your profile can still appear in Hush Line without it, but sources cannot send encrypted tips through your form until encryption is set up.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

Hush Line's directory now includes refreshed SecureDrop listings so you can search news organizations before you send anything sensitive.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

Hush Line's public directory now includes refreshed SecureDrop listings, so you can search news organizations and open the listing you need from one place. That makes it easier to verify where you're headed before you send anything sensitive.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
A tip line should explain itself before a source writes anything.

For admins and teams running Hush Line, the guidance settings let you add plain-language safety notes and an emergency exit button to the send flow.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: For admins: set guidance before sources send a message
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When a source lands on a tip line, they may still need simple instructions before they decide what to do.

For admins or teams running Hush Line, the guidance settings let you add that context in plain language. You can tell visitors to use Tor Browser, avoid a work device, or leave the page quickly with an emergency exit button.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
If you run Hush Line for a newsroom, law office, school, or organization, the first screen should answer basic safety questions.

The admin guidance settings let your team add plain-language instructions before a source sends anything, including cautions about work devices, Tor Browser, and an emergency exit button.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Admins can add plain-language safety instructions and an emergency exit option so visitors know how to approach a tip line more carefully.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:

Hush Line's Vision Assistant helps recipients turn those images into searchable text in the browser, without sending image data to Hush Line servers.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
If your team runs Hush Line, the directory should explain itself right away.

The admin branding settings let you add a plain-language intro so visitors know who runs the deployment and that no account is required to send a message.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: For admins: explain your deployment clearly
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When someone lands on your Hush Line deployment, they should be able to tell what it is and who it is for right away.

For admins and teams running Hush Line, the branding settings let you write a plain-language directory intro and set the visual identity around it. That helps visitors understand where they are, find the right recipient, and send a message without creating an account.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:

The admin branding settings let your team add a plain-language directory intro and match the deployment to your organization, so visitors know where they are before they choose a recipient or send a message.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Branding settings let admins set the directory intro and visual identity so visitors understand who runs the tip line and how to use it.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:

The admin registration settings let you turn signup off, keep it open, or require registration codes.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: For admins: control who can register
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
Not every Hush Line deployment should let anyone create an account.

For admins and teams running Hush Line, the registration settings let you disable signup, leave it open, or require registration codes. That gives a newsroom, law office, school, or internal reporting team more control over who gets recipient access.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
If your team runs Hush Line, account signup should match how the deployment is actually used.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
For admins and teams running Hush Line, account setup is part of the safety work.

This admin view helps operators review roles, trust flags, and whether users have 2FA or PGP enabled.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: For admins: review account trust in one place
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When a team runs Hush Line, the first question is not just who can sign in. It is who should be trusted to receive sensitive messages and how that access is being managed.

This admin view gives teams one place to review account roles, verification status, caution or suspension flags, and basic security signals like 2FA and PGP setup across users.

For admins and teams running Hush Line: check your admin settings before you publish or expand access. Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
For admins and teams running Hush Line, account setup is part of the safety work.

This admin view shows who is verified, who has admin access, which accounts are flagged, and how many users have 2FA or PGP enabled.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: The admin panel helps teams running Hush Line review roles, trust flags, and who has 2FA or PGP enabled across user accounts.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
When a reporting workflow changes, you should be able to leave with your records.

Hush Line's advanced settings let recipients download their data, including encrypted PGP messages, and close an account from one place.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Take your records with you if your setup changes
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When a reporting workflow changes, you may need to leave with your records instead of starting over.

Hush Line's advanced settings let recipients download a ZIP of their data, including encrypted PGP messages, and handle account closure from the same place. That gives journalists, lawyers, educators, and other trusted recipients a clearer exit path when they need to move work, archive it, or shut an account down.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
When a reporting workflow changes, you should be able to leave with your records.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
Sources should not need an account to send a tip.

For admins and teams running Hush Line, registration settings decide whether staff signup is off, open, or code-only.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: For admins: choose how staff accounts are created
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
Sources should not need an account to send a tip. For admins and teams running Hush Line, the separate question is how staff accounts get created.

Registration settings let you disable self-signup, leave it open, or require a registration code. That gives a newsroom, law office, school, or nonprofit a straightforward way to match account creation to the way the deployment is actually run.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
Sources should not need an account to send a tip. For admins and teams running Hush Line, the separate question is how staff accounts get created.

Registration settings let you turn self-signup off, leave it open, or require a code so staff access matches the way your deployment actually runs.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Registration settings let teams running Hush Line decide whether staff signup stays off, open, or code-only.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
Don't publish a half-finished tip line.

The last Hush Line onboarding step lets recipients decide whether to join the public directory now or wait until their profile, encryption key, and delivery settings are ready for sources.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Decide when your tip line goes public
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
A public tip line should not send sources to a half-finished profile.

The last step in Hush Line onboarding lets recipients choose whether to appear in the public directory now or wait until their profile, encryption key, and delivery settings are ready. That helps journalists, lawyers, educators, and other trusted recipients publish a listing only when a source can verify who they are and send a message with confidence.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
A public tip line should not send sources to a half-finished profile.

The last onboarding step lets recipients decide whether to join Hush Line's public directory now or wait until their profile, encryption key, and delivery settings are ready.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: The final onboarding step lets recipients join the public directory only when their profile, encryption, and delivery settings are ready for sources to use.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
If you need to contact a lawyer about a sensitive matter, it helps to narrow the list first.

Hush Line's public attorney directory lets sources filter by location, compare options, and verify who they are contacting before secure first contact. Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Filter the attorney directory before you reach out
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
If you need to contact a lawyer about a sensitive matter, the first problem is often figuring out which office fits your situation.

This Hush Line directory view lets sources narrow attorney listings by location before they reach out. It helps people compare options, verify who they are contacting, and start secure first contact without creating an account.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
If you need to contact a lawyer about a sensitive matter, it helps to narrow the list before you share anything.

Hush Line's public attorney directory lets sources filter by location, compare options, and verify who they are contacting. No account is required to send a message.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Public filters help sources narrow whistleblower-support attorneys by location before they contact a law office or share sensitive details.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
New-message alerts should reach the people who can review them.

Hush Line includes notification setup during onboarding, before recipients share a public tip line with sources.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Set tip alerts before your line goes public
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When a newsroom, law office, classroom, or security team opens a tip line, new-message alerts should reach the people who can review them.

Hush Line includes notification setup during onboarding so recipients can decide how new tips are surfaced before they share a public address with sources.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
When a newsroom, law office, classroom, or security team opens a tip line, new-message alerts should reach the people who can review them.

Hush Line includes notification setup during onboarding, before recipients share a public address with sources.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: The notification step helps recipients decide where new message alerts should go before sources start using the tip line.

recent_archive_history.bluesky_copy:
After someone sends a sensitive message, they should know what happens next.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text a sender sees during review, without turning the disclosure flow into live chat.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.headline: Set clear status text for incoming messages
recent_archive_history.linkedin_copy:
When someone sends a sensitive message, uncertainty can make the waiting period harder.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text a sender sees after submitting. A newsroom, law office, nonprofit, or other intake team can explain what happens next without turning the disclosure flow into live chat.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.mastodon_copy:
When someone sends a sensitive message, the waiting period should be clear.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text a sender sees after submitting, so a newsroom, law office, or intake team can explain what happens next during review.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
recent_archive_history.subtext: Recipients can edit the update a sender sees after submitting a message, so sources know what to expect while the disclosure is reviewed.
